evals.prompt: I'm starting a new order management system in Python. Can you help me set up the project structure?
evals.expected_output: The agent scaffolds the prescribed Clean Architecture folder layout under src/ (domain/, application/, adapters/, infrastructure/) and explains the dependency rule before writing any framework-specific code.
assertions: Creates src/domain/entities/ and src/domain/value-objects/ directories
Creates src/application/use-cases/, src/application/interfaces/, src/application/dto/ directories
Creates src/adapters/controllers/, src/adapters/presenters/, src/adapters/gateways/ directories
Creates src/infrastructure/persistence/, src/infrastructure/web/, src/infrastructure/external/, src/infrastructure/config/ directories
Mentions the Dependency Rule (source code dependencies point inward only)
Does not place business logic inside controllers or ORM models
References entities.md and use-cases.md for next steps

evals.prompt: Add a CreateOrder feature to my existing app. Users submit an order with items and a shipping address, we validate stock, then persist it.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads references/adding-features.md and references/use-cases.md, designs a CreateOrder use case named by intent, defines an OrderRepository port in application/interfaces/, and writes a failing test first using an in-memory fake of the port.
assertions: Use case file is named CreateOrder (intent-based), not OrderService or OrderCreator
Defines an OrderRepository port interface in the application layer
Provides an in-memory fake implementation for testing
Writes a failing test before the implementation (TDD red-green-refactor)
Does not import any framework, ORM, or database library inside the use case
Uses DTOs (not entities) to cross the use case boundary
Stock validation lives in the domain or use case, not in the controller

evals.prompt: Should my Order entity extend the Django model class so I can save it directly?
evals.expected_output: The agent explains that entities must not depend on the ORM or framework. The Order entity stays pure; a separate persistence model or mapper in the infrastructure layer handles database concerns.
assertions: Explicitly answers no — entities do not extend ORM model classes
Explains the dependency rule and how extending a framework class violates it
Proposes a mapper or adapter in infrastructure/persistence/ to translate between the entity and the ORM model
Mentions that the domain layer is framework-agnostic and testable without a database
References entities.md or repositories.md for deeper guidance

evals.prompt: How do I write tests for my AuthenticateUser use case? It depends on a UserRepository and a PasswordHasher.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads references/tdd.md and describes using in-memory fakes for ports rather than mocks. Entity tests need no test doubles. Use case tests wire fakes through the constructor/dependency injection.
assertions: Recommends in-memory fake implementations of UserRepository and PasswordHasher, not mocks
Shows fakes injected into the use case constructor or factory
Explains that use case tests run in-process with no database or HTTP
Notes that entity-only tests require no test doubles
Follows red-green-refactor: failing test first, then implementation
References tdd.md

evals.prompt: Our codebase has controllers/, models/, and views/ folders. Business logic is scattered across service classes. How do I migrate to Clean Architecture without rewriting everything?
evals.expected_output: The agent reads references/adding-features.md and references/boundaries.md, explains the strangler fig pattern, and outlines a use-case-at-a-time migration starting with the highest-value flow.
assertions: Recommends an incremental migration, not a full rewrite
Identifies one use case (one flow) to migrate first
Extracts entities from existing models into src/domain/entities/
Introduces a port interface before touching infrastructure
Keeps old controllers delegating to new use cases during transition
Mentions the Screaming Architecture test (folders should describe what the app does)
References adding-features.md or boundaries.md

evals.prompt: Can you recommend the best Node.js ORM for my project and write the database schema?
evals.expected_output: The agent reframes the question: the ORM is a detail that lives in infrastructure/persistence/. It first helps shape the domain entities and repository ports, then implements persistence behind those ports — making the ORM swappable.
assertions: Does not treat the ORM choice as the starting point
Designs entities and repository ports first, ORM choice second
Places any ORM code in src/infrastructure/persistence/ behind a repository interface
Explains that the database and ORM are plugins, not the core of the app
References repositories.md or infrastructure.md

evals.prompt: Write a PaymentService class that handles Stripe charges, sends receipts, updates the order status, and logs to Sentry.
evals.expected_output: The agent flags that PaymentService is doing too much. It proposes a single-purpose use case (e.g., ChargePayment) that depends on ports (PaymentGateway, NotificationService, OrderRepository, Logger) and implements each port in infrastructure.
assertions: Splits the work into a named use case (ChargePayment or similar intent-based name), not a generic Service class
Defines separate ports for payments, notifications, and logging
Places Stripe and Sentry clients in src/infrastructure/external/ as adapters implementing the ports
The use case does not import stripe or sentry packages directly
References solid.md (SRP) or adapters.md
